name: Strait of Hormuz
geopoliticalContext: ~20% of global oil supply and ~25% of LNG transits this strait daily. Iran controls the northern shore and has repeatedly threatened closure. IRGCN fast-attack craft and mining capability represent primary threat vectors. Closure would trigger immediate global energy crisis.

name: Bab-el-Mandeb
geopoliticalContext: Gateway between Red Sea and Gulf of Aden. Houthi forces have attacked >80 commercial vessels since Nov 2023, forcing major carriers to reroute via Cape of Good Hope (+10-14 days, +$1M per transit). US and allied forces conducting Operation Prosperity Guardian.

name: Strait of Malacca
geopoliticalContext: World's busiest chokepoint. ~40% of global trade and 90% of Japan/South Korea oil imports pass through the narrowest point. Piracy historically elevated. Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia trilateral patrol agreement. Depth constraints limit supertanker drafts.

name: Taiwan Strait
geopoliticalContext: ~50% of global container ship traffic transits annually. PLA conducts regular military exercises near the strait. TSMC fabs in Taiwan produce ~90% of advanced semiconductors. US commitment to Taiwan defense under TRA creates potential for great-power confrontation. Active PLA Navy and ROCN patrols.

name: Suez Canal
geopoliticalContext: Controlled by Egypt's Suez Canal Authority. Ever Given grounding (Mar 2021) blocked transit for 6 days, cost global trade ~$10B/day. Houthi attacks on Red Sea have reduced canal traffic ~42% since Dec 2023. New parallel lane completed 2015 allows two-way transit.

name: Panama Canal
geopoliticalContext: ~3% of global seaborne trade; critical for US East Coast-Asia container routes. 2024 drought reduced lake water levels, forcing draft restrictions and transit limits (~24/day vs normal 36). Chinese state company Hutchison Ports operates container terminals at both entrances. US-Panama treaty governs canal.

name: Strait of Gibraltar
geopoliticalContext: Entry/exit between Atlantic and Mediterranean. UK territory of Gibraltar controls northern shore; Morocco the south. NATO exercises frequently conducted here. Spanish territorial dispute with UK over Gibraltar remains active. Critical submarine surveillance point.

name: Lombok Strait
geopoliticalContext: Alternative deep-water passage to Malacca Strait used by supertankers and naval vessels with draft constraints. Chinese naval transits here to reach Indian Ocean have increased since 2020. Indonesian territorial waters; PLAN transits create diplomatic friction.
